Software Comparison

Procore vs. Knowify (2026)

Procore is built for the GC. Knowify is built for the sub. For commercial subcontractors, the right choice often comes down to who you are — not which platform is "better."

Procore and Knowify both serve the commercial construction market, but they target fundamentally different users. Procore is designed around the general contractor perspective — managing the whole project, coordinating subs, controlling documents, and reporting to the owner. Knowify is designed around the subcontractor perspective — managing your contract with the GC, billing on schedule, tracking job costs, and running your own business. This distinction drives almost every feature comparison.

Quick comparison

ProcoreKnowify
PricingCustom ($10K+/yr typical)~$149–$299/mo
Designed forGeneral contractorsSpecialty subcontractors
AIA billing (G702/G703)Basic (subcontractor facing)⭐ Purpose-built
Job costing vs. bidGood (GC perspective)⭐ Built for sub's contract
Change order tracking⭐ ComprehensiveStrong
Subcontractor management⭐ ComprehensiveManages your own subs
Owner/GC portal⭐ Full owner portalClient portal for GCs
Financial management⭐ Full (GC-level)Strong for sub financials
Plan management⭐ FullBasic
Field management⭐ Full suiteModerate
Best forCommercial GCs, large subsCommercial specialty subs

Procore's strengths

The GC's command center

Procore is designed to give the GC visibility and control over every aspect of a complex project — managing dozens of subcontractors, tracking RFIs and submittals, controlling the drawing set, and reporting to the owner. If you're a GC managing multiple commercial projects simultaneously, Procore's depth across bid management, contract management, financial management, and field management is hard to match.

Subcontractor management from the GC side

From a GC's perspective, Procore's subcontractor tools are comprehensive. You can issue subcontracts, track sub insurance compliance, manage sub pay applications, handle change orders with subs, and see sub schedule of values. This is the GC-facing view — built to manage hundreds of subcontractors across dozens of projects.

Financial breadth

Procore handles the full financial picture for a GC — owner contracts, subcontracts, purchase orders, change orders, pay applications, commitments, and budget forecasting. For GCs doing $10M+ in commercial work, this financial depth is one of the primary reasons to choose Procore.

Knowify's strengths

AIA billing built around the sub's workflow

Knowify's AIA billing module is purpose-built for subcontractors who submit G702/G703 applications for payment. The workflow generates accurate pay applications automatically — calculating percent complete, stored materials, retainage, and net payment — in the format GCs expect. Getting this right every billing cycle is critical to cash flow, and Knowify makes it fast.

Sub-perspective job costing

Knowify tracks costs against your contract value — not the GC's overall project budget. You see what you bid, what you've spent, and what your projected margin is. This is the financial view that matters for running your subcontracting business, and it's structured around your contract rather than the GC's project accounting.

Price and simplicity

Knowify starts at ~$149/month, which is a fraction of Procore's typical annual contract cost. For a specialty sub with 5–30 employees, this economic difference is significant. Knowify also has a faster implementation timeline — weeks rather than months — because it's focused on the sub's workflow rather than the full GC platform.

Designed for specialty trades

Knowify was built with electrical, mechanical, plumbing, HVAC, and other specialty subcontractors in mind. The contract structures, billing formats, and job costing categories align with how trade contractors actually operate — not how GCs think about managing their subs.

Can subcontractors use Procore?

Many commercial subs do use Procore — often because their GC clients require it for collaboration on projects (accessing drawings, submitting RFIs, logging daily work). In these cases, subs may have a Procore license for GC-required project access while still using a separate platform like Knowify to manage their own business operations.

Procore also offers a "Subcontractor" focused product tier, but most subs find that Knowify better reflects how they actually run their business, while Procore remains GC-centric even in its sub-facing features.

The right choice by profile

Choose Procore if:

  • You're a commercial GC managing multiple subs and need full project command
  • You're a large sub ($20M+) with complex financial management needs approaching GC-level
  • Your GC clients require Procore and you need a first-class Procore experience
  • You need the full Procore ecosystem (300+ integrations, owner portals, preconstruction)

Choose Knowify if:

  • You're a specialty subcontractor doing commercial project-based work
  • AIA billing and job costing against your contract value are your primary needs
  • You want professional subcontractor tools at a price that makes sense for a mid-size sub
  • You want to be operational quickly without a months-long implementation

Bottom line

For most commercial specialty subcontractors, Knowify is the better fit — lower cost, purpose-built for the sub workflow, and faster to get running. Procore is the right choice for GCs and very large subs who need enterprise-level project management depth and are willing to invest in the platform accordingly.

Affiliate disclosure: This page does not contain paid affiliate links to Procore or Knowify. We review all platforms independently. Read our full disclosure →
